---
title: "1.1 Introduction to Algorithms, Programming, and Compilers"
subtitle: "Part 1: Algorithms and First Java Program"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Why Java?

What do Android phones, Minecraft, and Netflix have in common? They're all coded in <span class="term">Java</span> (编程语言).

Many of the apps you use in an Android phone or tablet are written in Java.

Netflix uses Java for some of its software too. Java is a <span class="term">programming language</span> that is used worldwide to create software that we all use.

## Algorithms

### Step-by-step processes

<span class="term">Algorithms</span> (算法) define step-by-step processes in order to complete a task or solve a problem.

::: {.tight-list}
- A recipe is an algorithm for cooking a meal.
- A set of directions to a friend's house is an algorithm for getting there.
- In computer science, algorithms are used to solve problems and to create software.
:::

For example, an algorithm can be used to sort a list of numbers, to search for a word in a document, or to code a game.

## Algorithms

### Planning and sequencing

Algorithms can be used to plan and design code by writing the steps down in English or another language or in a diagram or in <span class="term">pseudocode</span> (伪代码), which is planning code on paper.

It is important to plan the algorithm step by step where <span class="mark">each step can be implemented by a line of code</span>.

<span class="term">Sequencing</span> (顺序执行) defines an order for when steps in a process are completed.

Steps in a process are completed <span class="mark">one at a time</span>.

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: brushalgorithm`

Textbook prompt: Put the following algorithm steps for brushing your teeth in order.

::: {.tight-list}
- Take the toothbrush.
- Put some toothpaste on the toothbrush.
- Put the brush in your mouth.
- Brush each section of your teeth.
- Rinse your mouth with water.
:::

## Correct Order

### `parsonsprob:: brushalgorithm`

```text
1. Take the toothbrush.
2. Put some toothpaste on the toothbrush.
3. Put the brush in your mouth.
4. Brush each section of your teeth.
5. Rinse your mouth with water.
```

## Student Response Task

### `shortanswer:: pbjalgorithm`

Textbook prompt: Write an algorithm for someone, maybe a robot, to make a peanut butter and jelly sandwich.

::: {.tight-list}
- Include at least <span class="mark">5 precise steps</span> in order.
- Have someone, maybe your instructor, act it out.
- Ask: Were your instructions precise enough?
:::

## First Java Program

### Class and main method

Every program in Java is written as a <span class="term">class</span> (类).

Java is an <span class="term">object-oriented language</span>, and classes and objects created from classes are the basic building blocks in object-oriented programming.

Inside the class, there can be a <span class="term">main method</span> that starts the program.

A <span class="term">method</span> (方法) is a block of code that performs a specific task.

## Java Program Template

### Simple class with `main`

```java
public class MyClass
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        // Put your code here!
    }
}
```

When you ask the Java run-time to run a class, it will always start execution in the `main` method.

## Brace Note

### Match `{` and `}`

In Java every open curly brace `{` must have a matched close curly brace `}`.

These are used to start and end class definitions and method definitions.

```java
public class MyClass
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        // Put your code here!
    }
}
```

## Code Task

### `activecode:: printName`

Textbook prompt: Run this code to see the output below it.

Then change the code to print your name, for example `"Hi Pat!"`, and run again.

Be sure to keep the starting `"` and ending `"`.

## Code Window

### `activecode:: printName`

```java
public class MyClass
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        System.out.println("Hi there!");
    }
}
```

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: printName`

Runestone's starter test expects this output from `main`:

```text
Hi there!
```

The task is still to edit the string and print your own greeting after running the starter.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- define an <span class="term">algorithm</span> as a step-by-step process
- explain <span class="term">sequencing</span> as completing steps one at a time in order
- describe <span class="term">pseudocode</span> as planning code on paper
- identify the <span class="term">class</span> and <span class="term">main method</span> in a Java program
- preserve matched curly braces `{` and `}` in a Java class
- run the `printName` starter before editing the string literal
:::

## End

### Continue to Part 2

[Next: Compiling, keywords, and syntax errors →](1-1-part-2-compiling-keywords-and-syntax-errors.html)
